mission.title: kube-vip: Upgrade to 0.8.5 results in invalid memory/nil pointer error
mission.description: Upgrade to 0.8.5 results in invalid memory/nil pointer error. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify kube-vip troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kube-vip deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-vip -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-vip
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-vip -n kube-vip --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `"invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference" (runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference)`

steps.title: Review kube-vip configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kube-vip configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kube-vip -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-vip
kubectl get configmap -n kube-vip -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kube-vip
```
After the upgrade from `0.8.4` -> `0.8.5` I now get an error when running kube-vip. 

This is running on a Raspberry Pi 5 (8GB) in a k3s cluster.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Upgrade to 0.8.5 results in invalid memory/nil pointer error
steps.description:
could you try this image?

```
ghcr.io/kube-vip/kube-vip:v0.8.5
```
or you can wait for the nightly build of the `main` branch.
```yaml
env:
        - name: address
          value: 192.168.1.100
        - name: vip_arp
          value: "true"
        - name: lb_enable
          value: "true"
        - name: port
          value: "6443"
        - name: vip_cidr
          value: "32"
        - name: cp_enable
          value: "true"
        - name: cp_namespace
          value: kube-system
        - name: vip_ddns
          value: "false"
        - name: svc_enable
          value: "false"
        - name: vip_leaderelection
          value: "true"
        - name: vip_leaseduration
          value: "15"
        - name: vip_renewd
```

steps.title: Confirm Upgrade to 0.8.5 results in invalid memory/nil… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-vip -n kube-vip --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kube-vip --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `"invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference" (runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference)` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
could you try this image?

```
ghcr.io/kube-vip/kube-vip:v0.8.5
```
or you can wait for the nightly build of the `main` branch.
codeSnippets:
env:
        - name: address
          value: 192.168.1.100
        - name: vip_arp
          value: "true"
        - name: lb_enable
          value: "true"
        - name: port
          value: "6443"
        - name: vip_cidr
          value: "32"
        - name: cp_enable
          value: "true"
        - name: cp_namespace
          value: kube-system
        - name: vip_ddns
          value: "false"
        - name: svc_enable
          value: "false"
        - name: vip_leaderelection
          value: "true"
        - name: vip_leaseduration
          value: "15"
        - name: vip_renewdeadline
          value: "10"
        - name: vip_retryperiod
          value: "2"
        - name: prometheus_server
          value: :2112

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kube-vip installed or the issue environment reproducible.
